_comment: LAT-2867 — EN ui_strings-overlay voor (1) de Langhe-PDF capture (LangheCaptureBlock.astro) en (2) de index-pagina's /en/streken/, /en/wijnroutes/, /en/wijnhuizen/. Zonder deze values valt t() terug op de NL-default en staat er op /en/ Nederlands. De capture-strings (langheCapture.*) voorkomen de gate-lek op /en/artikelen/langhe-piemonte-4-dagen-route/. Redactionele copy is een eerste pass door dev — Content Writer (LAT-2831) mag de teksten verfijnen; de door de ticket aangeleverde strings (Wine Regions / Terroir, grapes & tradition / Guide to Wine Travel / Wine Routes / Wine Estates / Producers & wineries / Send me the Langhe PDF / Email address / your@email.com) zijn 1-op-1 overgenomen. Seeden met: DIRECTUS_URL=... DIRECTUS_TOKEN=... node directus/scripts/seed-ui-strings-en.mjs directus/data/ui-strings-en-lat2867.json
langheCapture.label: Email address
langheCapture.a.koptekst: The complete trip planner as PDF
langheCapture.a.body: This article gives you the essentials. If you actually drive the route, there's more: phone numbers for the wineries, opening hours by season, and an overview of what each day costs. It's all in the compact Langhe PDF I send to everyone who signs up for The Brief.
langheCapture.a.subcopy: Every two weeks you'll also receive The Brief: on wine, travel and the people behind it. Unsubscribe anytime.
langheCapture.c.body: If you want to know where to begin in the Langhe: I wrote a compact trip planner. Route, wineries, hotels, budget. Sign up for The Brief and it's yours right away.
langheCapture.c.subcopy: Every two weeks The Brief follows: on wine and travel from first-hand experience.
streken.index.meta.title: Wine Regions, From Piedmont to the Mosel | VinoMartino
streken.index.meta.description: Discover Europe's great wine regions — terroir, grape varieties, climate and the best producers. In-depth guides for wine lovers.
streken.index.hero.label: Wine Regions
streken.index.hero.desc: Piedmont, Etna, Burgundy, Mosel — every region has its own logic of soil, climate and grape. Here I explain them the way I came to know them: by driving there myself.
streken.index.tier1.label: Travelled myself
streken.index.tier1.title: Regions I drove through myself
streken.index.tier1.desc: I wrote these guides after visiting in person. Not sure where to begin? Start with the four marked "Begin here".
streken.index.tier2.label: Editorial guides
streken.index.tier2.title: Guides by country
streken.index.tier2.desc: Carefully compiled from primary sources and local knowledge, grouped by country.
streken.index.empty.title: The guides are on their way
streken.index.empty.descPre: Piedmont, Etna, Burgundy, Mosel and Priorat are top of the list. I'd rather write them well than fast — in the meantime, start with the
wijnhuizen.index.meta.title: Wine Estates, Producers & wineries | VinoMartino
wijnhuizen.index.meta.description: Discover the estates behind the bottle, from headstrong Barolo producers to volcanic pioneers on Etna. Personal portraits of winemakers who truly matter.
wijnhuizen.index.hero.label: Wine Estates
wijnhuizen.index.hero.desc: Not the bottle, but the people behind it. Winemakers I've visited and spoken with, from old Piedmontese families to radical new-wave producers on Etna.
wijnhuizen.index.empty.title: The portraits are on their way
wijnhuizen.index.empty.descPre: Each estate gets one story, not a scorecard. The first portraits appear once I've done the visits. In the meantime, start with the
wijnroutes.index.meta.title: Wine Routes, Guide to Wine Travel | VinoMartino
wijnroutes.index.meta.description: Follow the world's finest wine routes, from the steep northern flank of Etna to the winding Mosel. Practical routes for wine lovers who set out themselves.
wijnroutes.index.hero.label: Wine Routes
wijnroutes.index.hero.desc: Routes I've driven myself — in the Fiat Panda, by train, sometimes with one suitcase too many. Day-by-day itineraries, places to sleep, and the producers worth calling two weeks ahead.
wijnroutes.index.map.title: All routes on the map
wijnroutes.index.empty.title: Routes are on their way
wijnroutes.index.empty.descPre: Langhe, Etna, Mosel and Wachau are first in line. In the meantime: the
wijnroutes.index.empty.descPost: already contain logistics details per region.
